name: History, Profiles, DPO, and GRPO Change Simulated-Student Turn Fidelity
statement: Duan et al. report that history-conditioned and profile-conditioned workflows outperform prompting-only and simpler fine-tuning baselines for turn-level student simulation, and ablations show that simulator DPO, profile-generator GRPO, and both knowledge and behavior profile sections contribute to fidelity.
population_or_context: Math tutoring dialogue simulation with prior learning histories.
time_horizon: arXiv v1 submitted 2026-05-28 and retrieved on 2026-06-02.
quantitative_note: Table 1 reports ProfileRL at Acts 0.644, Corr. 0.516, Errors 0.149, Cos. Sim. 0.700, ROUGE-L 0.296; Table 2 reports ProfileRL at Acts 0.605, Corr. 0.466, Errors 0.150, Cos. Sim. 0.689, ROUGE-L 0.271 versus Prompting Only at 0.502, 0.446, 0.109, 0.488, 0.145.
caveats: This is current preprint evidence and should be revisited after peer review or later arXiv versions.
The evaluated workflow uses a private math dataset and method packages with different base models across prompting and fine-tuning conditions.
evaluation_method: Five-fold cross-validation over turn-level acts, correctness, errors, cosine similarity, and ROUGE-L, with workflow and profile-section ablations.
reported_metrics: Table 1 and Table 2 method-by-metric results and ablations.
limitations: The evidence does not isolate all causal variables because history, profile structure, model family, and training workflow differ across conditions.
The private math dataset limits generalization to other subjects and response formats.
This finding should be labeled as preprint evidence in synthesis.

source_locator.section: arXiv PDF Sections 3-5, Table 1, Table 2, Appendix C, and Limitations
source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: The staged finding paraphrases the history-conditioned simulation setup, ProfileRL benchmark, and DPO/GRPO/profile ablations.
